preferred_translation: what one thinks about, one proliferates about
alternative_translations: what one thinks about, one proliferates around
discouraged_translations: what one thinks about, one conceptualizes
what one thinks about, one imagines
definition: MN 18 sequence line linking thinking to proliferation.
notes: MN 18 directly supports this formula. It keeps vitakka distinct from papañca. What is taken up in thought can then become the footing for proliferation; the line should not flatten that shift into one bland act of mental processing.

example_phrases.translation: what one thinks about, one proliferates about
example_phrases.notes: The transition from thinking into proliferation in MN 18.

authority_basis.scope: Supports the line connecting thinking to proliferation in the MN 18 sequence.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use 'what one thinks about, one proliferates about' as the default rendering when this formula is translated directly.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Use the controlled alternate only when English flow demands it. Do not flatten papañceti into imagination-language or generic conceptual activity.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents the formula from erasing the shift from taken-up thought into proliferation.
